steps.title: Set up the equation for x-intercepts
steps.content:
An x-intercept occurs where $y = 0$. Substituting into the function:
$$x^2 - 18x - 360 = 0$$
Since the answer choices are simple integers, backsolving is faster than solving algebraically.

steps.title: Test the choices using backsolving
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($-30$, 0):
$$(-30)^2 - 18(-30) - 360 = 900 + 540 - 360 = 1080 \neq 0$$
Fails. Testing choice A ($-12$, 0):
$$(-12)^2 - 18(-12) - 360 = 144 + 216 - 360 = 0$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
